message.content: {"rulings": [{"id": "17215435-32f0-4c2f-ba24-35071bc56852", "relation": "unrelated", "reason": "Audit logging and session cookie authentication are independent features; both can be true simultaneously."}, {"id": "dae89d9f-51f5-4ef4-87f4-6d4eb0349dea", "relation": "unrelated", "reason": "Adding audit logging is unrelated to the number of lanes the system runs; both facts can coexist."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze the new claim against each existing claim.

NEW CLAIM:
- observed: 2026-05-11
- kind: event
- claim: "Audit logging was added to the system."

CLAIM 1:
- id: 17215435-32f0-4c2f-ba24-35071bc56852
- observed: 2026-05-11
- kind: config
- claim: "Authentication is implemented using session cookies."

Analysis: The new claim is about audit logging being added. The existing claim is about authentication using session cookies. These are different features/aspects of the system. They can both be true at the same time. They are unrelated.

CLAIM 2:
- id: dae89d9f-51f5-4ef4-87f4-6d4eb0349dea
- observed: 2026-04-21
- kind: state
- claim: "The system currently runs only one lane."

Analysis: The new claim is about audit logging being added. The existing claim is about the system running only one lane. These are different aspects of the system. They can both be true at the same time. They are unrelated.

Both existing claims are about different topics (authentication and system lanes) than the new claim (audit logging). Neither contradicts the new claim, and neither is a restatement of it. They are simply unrelated facts about the system.
